narrative: FY2024-25 shows Tata’s strongest overall position in the combined theme, but not across all RPT metrics. Tata leads sales-to-related-parties at 13.04%, leads sustainability capex at 37%, and ties for rank 1 on R&D at 100%. However, Tata is still behind SAIL and Jindal on related-party investments, behind SAIL on loans/advances, and last on related-party purchases at 37.78%. SAIL remains the cleanest RPT concentration benchmark on investments, loans/advances, and purchases, reflecting its standalone public-sector structure and internalised plant/mine/unit footprint. Jindal is the strongest stainless peer on low purchase concentration and improving sustainability R&D, rising to 51.98%, supported by product-longevity, corrosion-resistance, import-substitution, and renewable/WHRB/ZLD-linked projects. JSW remains numerically high on RPT concentration but ties Tata on R&D. For Tata senior management, the headline is: FY2025 sustainability investment leadership is strong, sales-side RPT exposure is best-in-peer, but purchase-side and investment-side related-party concentration remain the key governance watchpoints.
